dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7366 IH: Dietary Supplement Regulatory Uniformity Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Langworthy introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to clarify and affirm the preemptive authority of the Food and Drug Administration over dietary supplement regulation, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Dietary Supplement Regulatory Uniformity Act.

section.header: Clarification of Federal preemption with respect to dietary supplement regulation
section.text: Section 403A of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 343–1) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: No State (or political subdivision thereof) may establish or continue in effect any requirement concerning a dietary supplement which is different from, or in addition to, or that is not otherwise identical with, a requirement applicable to a dietary supplement under this Act.

Upon application of a State or a political subdivision thereof, the Secretary may, by regulation and after notice and opportunity for a hearing, exempt from paragraph (1), under such conditions as may be prescribed in such regulation, a requirement of such State or political subdivision applicable to a dietary supplement if—

subparagraph.text: such requirement is more stringent than the applicable requirement under Federal law; or

clause.text: such requirement addresses a compelling local condition; and
compliance with such requirement does not render the dietary supplement out of compliance with Federal law.
